sentence: If I ______ more time, I would travel the world. (second conditional)
explanation: Second conditional: If + past simple. <If I had> expresses an unreal present situation.

sentence: The report ______ by the manager yesterday. (passive voice)
explanation: Past simple passive: <was/were> + past participle: <was written>.

sentence: She suggested ______ to the new restaurant. (gerund or infinitive)
explanation: After <suggest>, use the gerund (-ing form): <suggested going>.

sentence: He promised ______ me with the project. (gerund or infinitive)
explanation: After <promise>, use the infinitive: <promised to help>.

sentence: The meeting was ______ until next Monday. (phrasal verb: postpone)
explanation: The phrasal verb <put off> means to postpone or delay.

sentence: She ______ the problem after hours of work. (phrasal verb: solve)
explanation: The phrasal verb <figure out> means to solve or understand something.

sentence: We need to ______ a solution before the deadline. (phrasal verb: think of)
explanation: The phrasal verb <come up with> means to think of or produce an idea.

sentence: He ______ his father. They look exactly the same. (phrasal verb: resemble)
explanation: The phrasal verb <take after> means to resemble a family member.

sentence: Don't ______ the opportunity to study abroad. (phrasal verb: miss)
explanation: The phrasal verb <pass up> means to miss or not take an opportunity.

sentence: The whole project was a wild goose chase. This means it was a ______ effort.
explanation: The idiom <a wild goose chase> means a pointless or wasted search.

sentence: She spilled the beans about the surprise party. She ______ the secret.
explanation: The idiom <spill the beans> means to reveal a secret.

sentence: He's been burning the midnight oil. He's been ______ very late.
explanation: The idiom <burn the midnight oil> means to work or study late at night.

sentence: Don't beat around the bush. Just say it ______.
explanation: The idiom <beat around the bush> means to avoid saying something directly.

sentence: She's on the ball. She's very ______ and efficient.
explanation: The idiom <on the ball> means being alert, competent, and quick to act.

sentence: It cost an arm and a leg. It was very ______.
explanation: The idiom <cost an arm and a leg> means something is very expensive.

sentence: The develop______ of the new software took six months.
explanation: Adding <-ment> to <develop> forms the noun <development>.

sentence: Her kind______ made everyone feel welcome.
explanation: Adding <-ness> to <kind> forms the noun <kindness>.

sentence: The ______ of the word 'un-' means 'not'. (prefix meaning)
explanation: A <prefix> is added to the beginning of a word to change its meaning.

sentence: Adding 're-' to 'write' gives us ______, meaning to write again.
explanation: The prefix <re-> means 'again': <rewrite> = write again.

sentence: If she studied harder, she ______ pass the exam. (transform to conditional)
explanation: Second conditional result clause: <would> + base form.

sentence: The letter ______ by the secretary. (transform: 'The secretary wrote the letter' → passive)
explanation: Passive voice: object becomes subject + <was> + past participle.

sentence: He told me that he ______ tired. (transform: 'I am tired' → reported speech)
explanation: In reported speech, present <am> changes to past <was>.

sentence: She said she ______ help me the next day. (reported speech: 'I will help you tomorrow')
explanation: In reported speech, <will> changes to <would> and <tomorrow> to <the next day>.

sentence: The cake ______ eaten by the children. (transform to passive: 'The children ate the cake')
explanation: Past simple passive: subject + <was/were> + past participle.

sentence: If it ______ tomorrow, we'll stay home. (first conditional)
explanation: First conditional: If + present simple. <If it rains> expresses a real possibility.
